meta.label: 🏔 Alpine Loop
meta.sub: VENEZIA → SWITZERLAND → AUSTRIA → DOLOMITES → VENEZIA · 1 – 17 AUG 2026
meta.intro: Every leg mapped with its bucket-list anchor plus the worthwhile stops along the way — viewpoints, lake swims, towns, activities and hidden gems. Pricey stops show a splurge vs budget call. Tap any card or map pin for the full breakdown.

startCard.title: Pick up the van — Venice
startCard.meta: FRI 1 AUG · afternoon collection · stock groceries, fuel up

endCard.title: Return van AM → Rialto, Venice
endCard.meta: MON 17 AUG · Venice 17–19 · train to Milan 19th · fly Iceland 20th

presets.label: 🏔 The Grand Loop
presets.desc: All three countries — Switzerland, Austria & the Dolomites, a stop a night.

presets.label: 🌿 Easy Pace
presets.desc: Skip Switzerland. The same 16 nights spent slower across Austria, a Lake Bled detour & the Dolomites.

presets.label: 🇨🇭 Swiss & Dolomites
presets.desc: Switzerland + the Dolomites via the Stelvio Pass, with extra nights to linger instead of deeper Austria.

days.note: Ease into van life on Italy's biggest lake — a warm first night before the mountains begin.
days.sleep: Camping San Benedetto, Peschiera del Garda (lakeside van pitch, ~€42–55/night for 2 + van in Aug; book ~2 mths ahead)
why: Sirmione's thermal peninsula and Scaliger water-castle
Easy turquoise swims to shake off the flight
A soft, lively landing into van life
tips: Big cheap supermarket shop here — Swiss prices later will sting
Camping Butterfly / San Benedetto have lake-edge pitches; book ahead

stops.d: Storybook lakefront town on a thin peninsula, with sprawling Roman villa ruins at the tip.
Flat-rock shallows off Sirmione with impossibly clear turquoise water — the iconic Garda swim.
Fairytale medieval watermill hamlet on the river — a quiet detour most tourists miss.
Your Italy food night — lakefront trattoria, fresh lake fish and a glass of local Bardolino red.

plan.d: Storybook lakefront town on a thin peninsula — Scaliger water-castle + Grotte di Catullo (~€8pp). Park outside the old town; it is van-restricted.
MUST-DO. Flat-rock shallows off Sirmione with impossibly clear turquoise water. Free. Bring water shoes.
Hidden gem — fairytale medieval watermill hamlet on the river. Free. Quiet detour en route to the evening base.
MUST-DO food night. Lakefront trattoria — lake fish + local Bardolino red, ~€30pp. Big supermarket shop here too.

days.note: Transit over the Gotthard into Switzerland, then two wishlist thrills by the lake.
days.sleep: Camping Lido Luzern, Lidostrasse 19 (lakeside motorhome pitch ~CHF 52–68/night for 2 + van in Aug; 2.5 km from old town, next to the Lido swim; book ~2 mths ahead — fills fast on Aug weekends)
why: Fräkigaudi toboggan — Switzerland's longest summer run
Stoos ridge — cliff walk off the world's steepest funicular
Painted old town and the wooden Chapel Bridge
tips: Swiss vignette (CHF 40) mandatory — buy at the border
Cook in the van; Switzerland is eye-wateringly pricey

stops.d: 1,350m summer toboggan run down Fräkmüntegg — your wishlist thrill. Ride the cable car up.
Easy 2h cliff-edge ridge walk between Klingenstock and Fronalpstock, reached by the steepest funicular on earth.
14th-c covered bridge and the mournful carved lion — a free evening old-town wander.
Cool off in the lake at the city Lido or a free shoreline spot.

plan.d: Early roll-out to beat the Gotthard tunnel queue (or scenic Susten/Gotthard pass if the tunnel is jammed). ~4h. Buy the CHF 40 motorway vignette at the border. Stretch + coffee at Andermatt.
Switzerland's longest summer toboggan run at Fräkmüntegg, ~CHF 9/run — do two. Park the van free at the Kriens valley station; ride the gondola up. Great-value adrenaline before the city.
Walk the wooden Kapellbrücke, the old town squares and the Lion Monument — all free. Self-cater dinner at the van to offset Swiss restaurant prices.
MUST-DO. Golden-hour swim off Lido Luzern (~CHF 7) or a free shoreline spot, steps from the campsite. Cool off, then grill at the pitch.

days.note: Into the Jungfrau valley — 72 waterfalls off sheer walls. Your base for three nights, and your paragliding day.
days.sleep: Camping Jungfrau (Hobby 3), Lauterbrunnen (valley-floor van pitch facing Staubbach Falls ~CHF 56–72/night for 2 + van in Aug; walk to village + Wengen/Mürren lifts; book 2–3 mths ahead — sells out for peak Aug. Cheaper value option: Camping Schützenbach ~CHF 40–52/night, 1 km south)
why: The most jaw-dropping valley floor in the Alps
Tandem paragliding over the valley — your locked must-do
Cliff villages of Mürren & Wengen above
tips: Camping Jungfrau books months ahead for August — reserve now
Park and use cable cars/trains; valley driving is limited

stops.d: Tandem flight floating past waterfalls and cliffs — the signature Swiss adrenaline hit and your locked must-do.
300m freefall waterfall drifting in the wind right above the village — a short free walk behind it.
Ten glacier waterfalls thundering *inside* the mountain, reached by a tunnel lift. Eerie and unforgettable.
Car-free timber village on a ledge with full Eiger-Mönch-Jungfrau views; great fondue dinner here.

plan.d: ~1h drive over the Brünig Pass to Camping Jungfrau. Check in, level the van, then go car-free for the valley — paid parking in Lauterbrunnen is dear.
Free 297m free-falling waterfall right behind the village; short path lets you walk behind the spray. Easy warm-up with the Jungfrau wall ahead.
Ten glacier-fed falls thundering INSIDE the mountain, reached by tunnel-lift (CHF 14pp). Genuinely unique and weatherproof. Light lunch from the van after.

MUST-DO. Cable car from Stechelberg/Lauterbrunnen (~CHF 12) up to car-free Mürren for the classic Eiger-Mönch-Jungfrau balcony view; stay for a sunset fondue (~CHF 28pp) before the last cable car down.

days.note: The big one — 'Top of Europe'. Or swap the steep ticket for a cheaper, hike-rich alternative.
days.sleep: Camping Jungfrau (Hobby 3), Lauterbrunnen — night 2 of 2 (stay put, van already parked; ~CHF 56–72/night for 2 + van in Aug). Keeping the same valley base saves a teardown/relocate and lets you catch the first train up to Jungfraujoch/First. Book both nights together 2–3 mths ahead — peak-Aug sells out. Value swap: drop 1 km to Camping Schützenbach ~CHF 40–52/night if Jungfrau is full.

badges.t: Splurge stop — pick a clear day

why: Europe's highest railway station at 3,454m
Ice Palace inside the glacier + Sphinx deck
The vast Aletsch glacier below you
tips: First train = clear morning views before cloud builds
Only worth it on a bluebird day — check the webcams

stops.d: Cogwheel through the Eiger to the 3,454m saddle, ice palace and glacier panorama.
stops.bu: Stop at Kleine Scheidegg (Eiger face) or ride Männlichen ~CHF 60pp

stops.d: Budget alternative: cable car to First, then easy 1h walk to the mirror-still Bachalpsee. Half the price, arguably better.
Detour to the electric-turquoise lake — swim at Iseltwald or Bönigen on the way back.

plan.d: MUST-DO splurge (~CHF 230pp return; book the FIRST train + a Half-Fare/GA discount to soften it). Cog railway from Lauterbrunnen via Kleine Scheidegg to the 3454m saddle — Aletsch Glacier, Ice Palace, snow even in August. Go early before cloud builds.

If energy + budget allow: Grindelwald-First gondola (~CHF 75pp) then the easy 1h walk to the mirror-still Bachalpsee. Skip without guilt if Jungfraujoch already maxed the day — it repeats tomorrow's region.
MUST-DO and FREE. Drive the van 30min to Iseltwald for a turquoise glacier-melt swim off the jetty as the light goes gold. Pay-park at the village lot; perfect reset after the railway crowds.

days.note: A big-views play day: cliff walks, scooters, ziplines and a revolving Bond summit.
days.sleep: Camping Eigernordwand, Grindelwald-Grund (valley-floor van pitch right under the Eiger north face ~CHF 50–66/night for 2 + van in Aug; 600 m walk to the Grindelwald Terminal for the First gondola & Eiger Express; book 2–3 mths ahead — peak-Aug fills. Value swap: Camping Gletscherdorf ~CHF 44–58/night, smaller & quieter, 1.2 km east of the village)
why: First Cliff Walk cantilevered over the void
The Trottibike scooter descent (your wishlist)
Schilthorn's Bond-film revolving restaurant
tips: Do First OR Schilthorn — both in a day is rushed
Trottibikes are first-come at the Bort mid-station

stops.d: Glass-floored cliff walkway, then zip back toward Grindelwald on the First Flyer.
Kick-scooter the winding mountain road down from Bort — your wishlist, pure fun.
Revolving 360° summit restaurant from On Her Majesty's Secret Service.
stops.bu: Ride only to Birg mid-station for the thrill walk ~CHF 70pp

stops.d: Vineyard-backed bay at Spiez — a gentler, warmer swim than the glacier lakes.

plan.d: First gondola up from Grindelwald-Grund (walkable from Camping Eigernordwand). Free Tissot Cliff Walk over the void, then the First Flyer zipline (~CHF 31) screaming back toward the valley. Go early for clear Eiger views.
MUST-DO and great value (~CHF 19). Grab a Trottibike at Bort and carve the 4.5km back-road down to Grindelwald — the best fun-per-franc on the whole loop.
Only if you skipped Jungfraujoch — the cheaper 360° Bond-film summit above Mürren. Otherwise drop it and head straight to the lake; two big lift days back-to-back is overkill on budget and legs.
MUST-DO and FREE. End the Berner Oberland with a warm lake swim and a vineyard-castle backdrop at Spiez. Cook dinner lakeside before the last night under the Eiger.

days.note: Car-free village beneath the Matterhorn — park at Täsch and shuttle up.
days.sleep: Camping Attermenzen, Randa (the working gateway pitch for car-free Zermatt, between the Randa & Täsch stations ~CHF 33–46/night for 2 + van in Aug, renovated 2021 with ~320 power sockets & a proper motorhome service point; trains run into Zermatt every ~20 min from Täsch/Randa, or the campsite shuttle-taxi ~CHF 7 each way; book 2–3 mths ahead for peak Aug). Note: the old Camping Alphubel in Täsch is permanently closed (rockfall/flood hazard), so Attermenzen is the realistic van base — leave the van here two nights and day-trip Zermatt/Gornergrat.
why: First look at the Matterhorn over the rooftops
Charming car-free Walliser village
Gateway to Gornergrat and the glaciers
tips: Vans park at the Matterhorn Terminal in Täsch (~CHF 16/day)
Camping Alphubel in Täsch is the practical base

stops.d: The classic shot — Matterhorn framed down the river from the church bridge, best at sunrise.
Funicular to a little lake that mirrors the Matterhorn — and you can actually swim in it.
Boardwalks through a deep marbled gorge just outside town — a cool, quiet hour.
Your Swiss cheese moment — bubbling raclette or fondue in the village.

plan.d: ~2.5h drive to Camping Attermenzen, Randa (Zermatt is car-free). Pitch the van, then take the train from Randa/Täsch into Zermatt (~20min, ~CHF 7 from Täsch P+R). Lunch from the camp shop to save on Zermatt prices.
FREE. The iconic photo of the Matterhorn framed down the church-bridge in the village centre. Grab it on arrival before afternoon cloud caps the peak.
MUST-DO. Underground funicular (~CHF 27pp) up to Leisee — a small alpine lake with a flawless Matterhorn reflection and a free swim/paddle. The best-value high view in Zermatt.
Splurge-but-worth-it Valais raclette (~CHF 35pp) before the last evening train back to Randa. Check the train timetable so you don't miss the connection to the van.

days.note: The Gornergrat railway and the most famous peak in the Alps — Switzerland's finale.
days.sleep: Camping Attermenzen, Randa — night 2 of 2 (van stays put, you parked it here yesterday ~CHF 33–46/night for 2 + van in Aug; drive=0 today). Keeping the same gateway base means a teardown-free morning and the first train up to Gornergrat: walk/shuttle to Täsch (~5 min), park-and-ride into car-free Zermatt every ~20 min, then the Gornergrat Bahn cog railway (book the GGB online ~CHF 88/person return — sit on the RIGHT going up for the Matterhorn; a Swiss Travel Pass gets ~50% off). Value tip: an early ascent beats the cloud and the crowds, and the Riffelberg→Riffelsee walk back down (free) gives the classic Matterhorn-reflection shot. Book both Attermenzen nights together 2–3 mths ahead for peak Aug. Cheaper swap if Attermenzen is full: Camping Mischabel in Saas-Grund ~CHF 30–42/night, ~40 min drive.
why: Cog railway to a 3,089m, 29-peak panorama
The Riffelsee Matterhorn reflection
Easy downhill alpine hike back
tips: Morning light is best on the Matterhorn's east face
Ride up, hike down a stage to save francs and see more

stops.d: Switzerland's highest open-air cog railway to a glacier-ringed summit terrace.
stops.bu: One-way up, hike down via Riffelberg ~CHF 66pp

stops.d: Short walk from Rotenboden to the tarn that mirrors the Matterhorn perfectly — the postcard.
Europe's highest cable-car station at 3,883m with an ice palace — for serious splurgers.

plan.d: Van stays put (2nd night at Camping Attermenzen, drive=0). Lazy breakfast, fill water, pack a picnic from the camp shop, then walk to Randa/Täsch station for the P+R train up to Zermatt — no teardown today.

First trains up from Zermatt; sit on the RIGHT going up for the Matterhorn. Best value = one-way up (~CHF 66pp) with Swiss Travel Pass ~50% off, then walk down. Glacier-ringed summit terrace at 3,089m.

FREE once up. Hop off at Rotenboden and walk the short trail to the Riffelsee tarn for the mirror-Matterhorn postcard, then continue the easy descent to Riffelberg for the train back — saves a full return fare.

Stroll car-free Bahnhofstrasse, photo from Kirchbrücke, then a value Valais raclette/rösti (~CHF 30pp) before the last evening train back to Randa. Check the timetable so you don't miss the connection to the van.

days.note: The long haul east. Break it with turquoise lakes and a tiny country, arrive Innsbruck by evening.
days.sleep: Camping Innsbruck Kranebitten, Kranebitter Allee 214 (the city's only proper campsite — riverside van pitches on the Inn under the Nordkette, ~€36–46/night for 2 + van in Aug; bus O/J ~15 min into the old town, on-site bar/restaurant; book ~1–2 mths ahead. Value/quiet swap: Stellplatz Völs or Camping Natterer See ~7 km SW ~€40–52)
why: Links the Swiss and Austrian Alps
Stunning Fernpass lakes to break the drive
Optional Liechtenstein country-tick
tips: Start early to beat A12 holiday traffic
Austria needs its own vignette — sort it before the border
days.cost: Austria vignette ~€12 · fuel is the main cost

stops.d: Tick off a whole country in an afternoon — castle above the vineyards, passport stamp at the tourist office.
Jewel-turquoise lakes right by the Fernpass road — the perfect mid-drive swim and picnic.
Famous ski town for a coffee-and-strudel leg stretch over the Arlberg pass.

plan.d: The long haul east (~4.5h total). Roll out early; coffee-and-strudel stop in Vaduz, Liechtenstein — tick a whole country, €3 passport stamp at the tourist office. Re-stock cheap groceries before the Austrian border.
MUST-DO. Jewel-turquoise lakes right by the Fernpass road — the perfect free mid-drive swim and van picnic. Small parking fee only; the cold clear water is the best break on the whole transit.
Arrive Camping Innsbruck Kranebitten on the Inn under the Nordkette (2-night base, van stays put tomorrow). Pitch, plug in, book the bus O/J into town (~15min).
FREE stroll: Golden Roof, medieval lanes, Inn-bridge sunset on the painted houses. Cheap first-night dinner at a bräustübl before the proper Tyrolean food night tomorrow.

days.note: Imperial old town wrapped in mountains, with a cable car straight to 2,000m.
days.sleep: Camping Innsbruck Kranebitten, Kranebitter Allee 214 — night 2 of 2 (van stays put, drive=0). Riverside van pitches on the Inn under the Nordkette ~€36–46/night for 2 + van in Aug; on-site bar/restaurant + bakery van, bus O/J ~15min into the old town. Teardown-free morning: walk to the bus and ride the Hungerburg funicular to the Nordkette. Book both nights ~1–2mo ahead. Value/quiet swap: Camping Natterer See ~7km SW ~€40–52 (lake swimming) or Stellplatz Völs.
why: Golden Roof and pastel old town
Nordkette — city centre to alpine ridge in 20 min
Architectural funicular by Zaha Hadid
tips: Nordkette funicular leaves from the centre — no car needed
Climb the City Tower for the rooftop-and-peaks view

stops.d: Zaha Hadid funicular + cable cars from downtown to a 2,256m ridge over the city.
stops.sp: All the way to Hafelekar ridge ~€44pp
stops.bu: Just to Hungerburg terrace for the view ~€10pp

stops.d: Gilded oriel, medieval lanes, Hofkirche — a free wander through Habsburg history.
Glide up the futuristic Olympic jump tower for a panorama and a coffee at the top.
Austria food-night option — Stiftskeller beer garden, schnitzel and a Tyrolean beer.

plan.d: Van stays put (drive=0). Zaha Hadid funicular from downtown straight up: best value = just the Hungerburg terrace (~€10pp) for the city-over-mountains view; splurge all the way to Hafelekar ridge at 2,256m (~€44pp) only on a clear morning.
FREE wander — gilded oriel, Hofkirche, medieval lanes. Grab a cheap leberkäse or market lunch instead of a sit-down to keep the day value-optimised.
Glide up the futuristic Olympic jump tower (~€11pp) for a panorama and coffee at the top — skip if Nordkette already gave you the big view and save the cash.
MUST-DO Austria food night — Stiftskeller beer garden: schnitzel, kaspressknödel and a Tyrolean beer (~€25pp). Walk back to the bus; van is already pitched at Kranebitten.

days.note: Mozart's baroque city — clifftop fortress, domes, beer gardens and Sound of Music.
days.sleep: Camping Nord-Sam, Salzburg (Samstrasse 22A, NE edge of the city ~€38–50/night for 2 + van in Aug; small friendly site with a pool, bus line 6/21 ~20 min to the old town & Mirabell, easy A1 roll-in after Innsbruck; book ~1 mth ahead — Aug fills on weekends). Value/quiet swap: Panorama Camping Stadtblick on the Rauchenbichl hill ~€36–48/night with a postcard fortress view, or the cheaper bare Salzburg city Stellplatz (no facilities, walk to the bus).
why: Hohensalzburg, a vast intact medieval fortress
UNESCO baroque old town and Mozart's house
Monastery beer hall — your Austria food night
tips: Park & ride on the edge; the centre is tight for vans
Funicular up the fortress beats the summer climb

stops.d: Clifftop castle over the city — ramparts, princely rooms and rooftop panoramas.
Wrought-iron signs, Mozart's birthplace, Domquartier squares — free to roam.
Cavernous monastery beer garden where you pour your own stein under chestnut trees — your locked Austria food night.
Playful 400-year-old water-joke gardens — silly, soaking, great on a hot day.

plan.d: Easy ~2h A1 roll-in. Pitch first at Camping Nord-Sam (pool, bus 6/21 ~20min to old town), then bus into the centre so the van is parked for the day.
Clifftop castle over the city — ramparts, princely rooms and rooftop panoramas (~€16pp incl. funicular). Best value = walk up the path and pay the cheaper walk-up ticket if your legs are willing.
FREE to roam — wrought-iron signs, Mozart's birthplace, Domquartier squares, Mirabell gardens for the Sound of Music shot. Picnic lunch in the gardens to save.
MUST-DO locked Austria beer-garden night — pour your own stein from the barrel and pick cheap deli food from the stalls under the chestnut trees (~€20pp). Bus back to Nord-Sam.

days.note: The lake-village postcard come to life — go early, before the day-trippers.
days.sleep: Camping am See, Obertraun (Winkl 77, directly on Lake Hallstatt's quiet south shore ~€34–46/night for 2 + van in Aug; own swim beach, and the Dachstein/Krippenstein Five Fingers cable car is ~5 min away — far better value than squeezing into tiny Hallstatt itself). Take the ~10 min ferry or the lakeside cycle path into Hallstatt village early, before the day-trippers. Book ~1–1.5 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swap: Camping Klausner-Höll right in Hallstatt is closer but cramped & pricier (~€44–56); Bad Goisern's Camping Lambichl ~€32–42 is the cheapest base if both lakefronts are full.
why: The world's most photographed alpine village
Oldest salt mine on earth + Skywalk
A ring of swimmable Salzkammergut lakes
tips: Day-tour buses hit ~10am — arrive at opening or stay till evening
Park at P1/P2 or stay in Obertraun across the lake

stops.d: Wander the lanes and hit the famous Classic Hallstatt viewpoint — completely free.
Funicular up to the oldest salt mine and the "Welterbeblick" platform jutting over the lake.
stops.bu: Just the Skywalk viewpoint via funicular ~€20pp

stops.d: Mirror lake reflecting the Dachstein glacier — a stunning, quiet free swim nearby.
Hand-shaped platform over a sheer drop on Krippenstein — vertigo and glory.

plan.d: Short ~1.5h drive; arrive Lake Hallstatt early and ferry/cycle in before the tour buses. Pitch is at Camping am See, Obertraun (own swim beach) — drop the van there first, the village is car-restricted.
FREE. Wander the lanes and hit the famous Classic Hallstatt viewpoint while it's still quiet — the whole reason to come early. Bakery breakfast beats the pricey cafés.
MUST-DO. Short drive to the Vorderer Gosausee — a mirror lake reflecting the Dachstein glacier, a stunning quiet FREE swim and lakeside picnic. The best-value highlight of the day.
From Obertraun, Krippenstein cable car (~€40pp) to the hand-shaped platform over a sheer drop — splurge only on a clear afternoon, otherwise swim from the camp beach for free and save it.

days.note: Austria's greatest drive — 36 hairpins to 2,500m, ending on Lake Zell.
days.sleep: Seecamp Zell am See, Thumersbach (Thumersbacher Strasse 34, right on the lake's east shore ~€42–56/night for 2 + van in Aug; own swim jetty + restaurant, and a sunset view back across the water to the town and the Kitzsteinhorn glacier). You arrive after running the Grossglockner High Alpine Road (toll ~€40/car, buy online & it's valid all day), so a lakefront pitch is the payoff. Book 1–2 mths ahead — peak Aug fills. Value swap: Sportcamp Woferlgut in Bruck ~10 min west ~€38–50/night (huge wellness site, great in rain), or the cheaper Panorama Camp Zell ~€34–44 on the hillside.
why: 36 hairpins to 2,504m at Hochtor
Glacier and highest-peak views at Franz-Josefs-Höhe
Marmots, ibex and alpine meadows
tips: Toll (~€39) includes same-day re-entry — go early
Low gear on the descents; it's steep but well-built

stops.d: The road's climax — staring straight at the Pasterze glacier and the Grossglockner summit.
Short cobbled spur to the road's highest point — a 360° sea of peaks.
Optional detour to Europe's tallest waterfall — a misty forest trail beside 380m of falls.
End the day with a swim and a lakeside dinner on the Zeller See.

plan.d: Drive south to the Grossglockner High Alpine Road. ~€40/car toll — but it's the day's whole point, so it's the value pick. Optional first stop: Krimml Waterfalls (~€8pp), Europe's tallest, a misty forest trail beside 380m of falls.

The road's climax — stare straight at the Pasterze glacier and the Grossglockner summit (toll-incl., parking on site). Picnic from the van with the glacier in front of you; marmots in the meadows below.
Toll-incl. short cobbled spur to the road's highest point (2,571m) — a 360° sea of peaks. Then descend the 36 famous hairpins toward Zell am See.
MUST-DO. Pitch at Seecamp Zell am See, Thumersbach (swim jetty), then end the day with a FREE swim and a lakeside dinner on the Zeller See — glacier sunset over the water.

days.note: Drop south into South Tyrol — Italy with a German accent and dolomite spires.
days.sleep: Camping Olympia, Toblach/Dobbiaco (Via Major 28, a big well-run year-round site on the valley floor ~€40–54/night for 2 + van in Aug; pool, sauna, on-site shop/restaurant and a direct cycle path to Lago di Dobbiaco — an ideal first Dolomites base after the long drive from Zell am See). Bus 442/445 and the Dolomiti SuperSummer mobility card put Tre Cime and Cortina within reach without moving the van. Book 1–2 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swap: Camping Toblacher See ~€36–46 right on the little lake, or Niederdorf's Stellplatz/agriturismo pitches if Olympia is full.
why: The handover from Austria into South Tyrol
Easy first dolomite lakes and valleys
Speck, dumplings and a great campsite base
tips: Restock in Bruneck/Toblach; valley shops are tiny
Camping Olympia Toblach is top-rated and well-serviced

stops.d: Sunny Tyrolean market town on the way down — arcaded streets and a castle.
Calm reed-fringed lake with an easy loop and a swim spot below the peaks.
Pretty pastel village with a Romanesque collegiate church — gelato stop.
Speck, canederli dumplings and a Lagrein red — your South Tyrol food night begins.

days.note: The emerald lake of every postcard, then the most iconic hike in the Dolomites.
days.sleep: Camping Olympic, Cortina d'Ampezzo (Località Fiames, on the SS51 ~4 km N of Cortina ~€44–58/night for 2 + van in Aug; pine-shaded riverside pitches, bus stop at the gate into Cortina, and you're already pointed at Misurina/Tre Cime for an early start before the Lago di Braies vehicle ban kicks in at 9:30). Today's itinerary drive is 0 — but this is a relocate from the Toblach base toward Cortina, so leave Olympia early and arrive at Braies before 9 am. Book 1–2 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swap: Camping Cortina (Località Campo) slightly closer to town ~€46–60, or the cheaper lakeside pitches up at Misurina if you want to wake under the Sorapis cliffs.
why: Lago di Braies — turquoise water and wooden rowboats
Tre Cime di Lavaredo — the three towers loop
Misurina lake for the golden-hour reflection
tips: Braies road is closed to cars ~9:30–16:00 in summer — be there before 9am
Tre Cime toll road to Rifugio Auronzo saves a long climb

stops.d: Row a wooden boat across glassy turquoise water beneath sheer cliffs. Dawn is magic and crowd-free.
The Dolomites' signature ~4h circuit around the three towers, past rifugios and WWI tunnels. Moderate, mostly level.
Lakeside resort that turns gold at sunset; gentle stroll and a paddle.
Tiny mirror lake on the Tre Cime road — the reflection shot everyone misses.

days.note: Seceda's tilted knife-edge ridge, alpine meadows, and the great Dolomite passes.
days.sleep: Camping Marmotta, Pozza di Fassa (Strada de Sèn Jan, in Val di Fassa ~30 min S of Ortisei over the passes ~€38–52/night for 2 + van in Aug; quiet riverside pitches, pool, on the bus line for the Catinaccio/Vajolet lifts — a great-value Dolomites base on the far side of the Sella loop you drive today). Day-trip the Seceda cable car from Ortisei and the Alpe di Siusi gondola, then drop down here over the Pordoi/Sella passes for the wine-country last night. Book 1–2 mths ahead for peak Aug. Value swaps: Camping Vidor in Pozza (large, full facilities) ~€40–54, or — if you'd rather end in the city — Camping Moosbauer in Bolzano ~€42–56 with a direct bus to the centre and an easy A22 launch to Venice next morning.
why: Seceda's surreal tilted ridgeline
Alpe di Siusi — Europe's largest alpine meadow
Driving the Sella & Pordoi passes
tips: Ortisei–Seceda cable car for fast morning light
Loop a couple of Sella Ronda passes if you've energy

stops.d: Cable car to the otherworldly tilted ridge above Ortisei — one of the great Dolomite views.
stops.bu: Drive to Alpe di Siusi base + meadow walk ~€18 bus

stops.d: Rolling green high plateau under the Sassolungo spires — easy meadow trails, best at sunrise.
Woodcarving capital of Val Gardena — pretty pedestrian centre and gelato.
The "rainbow lake" reflecting the Latemar — a quick stunner on the way toward Bolzano.
Your big food/wine night — a Gardena hut or a Bolzano-area winery: Gewürztraminer and Schlutzkrapfen.

days.note: A slow mountain morning, then an easy roll back to the lagoon for the hand-back.
days.sleep: Camping Jolly delle Querce, Marghera/Mestre (Via Giuseppe de Marchi 7, the closest proper campsite to the Indie Campers Venice depot ~€36–50/night for 2 + van in Aug; pool + restaurant, ACTV bus into Piazzale Roma ~15–20 min for a last lagoon evening). This is your calm pre-hand-back night — dump the grey/black tanks, refill water, and clean out the van here so the 11:00 return next morning is stress-free. Book ~1 mth ahead. Value swap: Camping Venezia (Marghera) or the cheaper Camping Fusina on the lagoon edge ~€34–46 with its own vaporetto to Venice — pick by which is nearer your specific depot.
why: Final dolomite views before the plains
A sparkling-wine detour through the Prosecco hills
Calm overnight near the depot
tips: Refuel and empty the van's tanks before drop-off
Camping Jolly/Venezia in Mestre is close to depot + Venice bus

stops.d: UNESCO vineyard ridges — a last Italian lunch with a glass of proper Prosecco straight from the source.
Canals, frescoed porticoes and tiramisù's birthplace — a relaxed final town stop.
Settle near the depot, empty tanks, repack — ready for the easy morning hand-back.

days.note: A tiny-country leg-stretch on the long Zermatt→Innsbruck drive.
days.sleep: (en route)
why: Tick off a whole country in an afternoon
Vaduz castle above the vineyards
Passport souvenir stamp
tips: Worth ~1–2h; it's a stop, not a stay
Right on the Switzerland→Austria route

stops.d: Walk up to the princely castle gates for a vineyard-and-valley view.
Get the novelty Liechtenstein stamp at the tourist office.

days.note: The most spectacular driving pass in the Alps — 48 hairpins of pure theatre.
days.sleep: (en route)
why: 48 stacked hairpins to 2,757m
Glacier and ridgeline views from the top
A rite of passage between Switzerland and the Dolomites
tips: Big vans manage it but go early — it's slow and busy
Can close for weather even in summer; check first

stops.d: Stand at the top of the hairpin wall with a bratwurst and a coffee.
The famous bend looking back down all 48 switchbacks.

days.note: South Tyrol's capital — a relaxed city rest-day with the world's oldest iceman.
days.sleep: Bolzano
why: Ötzi the 5,300-year-old iceman
Porticoed streets, Italian-Tyrolean blend
Best apple strudel and a gentle day
tips: Great rest day between hikes
Ritten cable car + narrow-gauge train for a half-day

stops.d: Meet the astonishingly preserved Copper-Age iceman and his gear.
Glide above the city to earth-pyramid hoodoos and meadow walks.

days.note: A fairytale island church and clifftop castle — an easy detour east of Austria.
days.sleep: Bled
why: Island church in a glacial lake
Clifftop Bled Castle
Cheaper than the Alps — and gorgeous
tips: Row a traditional pletna boat to the island
Pairs if you swing south from Salzburg/Grossglockner

stops.d: Row out to the island church, ring the wishing bell, swim off the boat.
Clifftop medieval castle over the red rooftops and turquoise water.
Boardwalk through an emerald river gorge a short drive away.

ideas.title: Lake Bohinj
ideas.desc: Bled's bigger, wilder, emptier sister — glacial water ringed by peaks, far fewer crowds. Pair with Vintgar Gorge.

ideas.title: Königssee
ideas.desc: Electric-green fjord lake reached by silent electric boats, an echoing trumpet stop, and the red-domed St Bartholomä chapel.

ideas.title: Lake Como
ideas.desc: Palm-lined promenades and villa gardens — ferry-hop between Bellagio, Varenna and Menaggio. A glamorous detour south.

ideas.title: Lake Maggiore
ideas.desc: The Borromean Islands float palaces and terraced gardens on a deep-blue lake straddling Italy and Switzerland.

ideas.title: Eibsee
ideas.desc: A turquoise forest lake at the foot of Germany's highest peak — rent a rowboat or walk the easy 7km shoreline loop.

ideas.title: Achensee
ideas.desc: Tyrol's largest lake, a fjord-like ribbon of teal water with a heritage steam cog railway and crisp alpine swims.

ideas.title: Wolfgangsee
ideas.desc: Storybook lake villages St Wolfgang and St Gilgen, with the Schafberg cog railway climbing straight off the shore.

ideas.title: Traunsee
ideas.desc: Austria's deepest lake under the Traunstein cliff, with the fairytale Orth Castle on its own little islet.

ideas.title: Lago di Resia
ideas.desc: The half-submerged 14th-c church bell tower rising from turquoise water — surreal, quiet, and unforgettable.

ideas.title: Lake Lugano
ideas.desc: A palm-fringed Mediterranean lake city tucked into the Swiss-Italian border — funiculars, lakefront, and easy day-trips.

ideas.title: Lake Iseo & Monte Isola
ideas.desc: The largest lake-island in Europe rises car-free from the water — quiet fishing villages and a sleeper among the famous lakes.

ideas.title: Lake Orta & Isola San Giulio
ideas.desc: The most romantic small lake — a basilica island, a frescoed Sacro Monte, and almost no crowds.

ideas.title: Passo Giau
ideas.desc: The most theatrical Dolomite pass — 29 hairpins to a meadow saddle framed by Ra Gusela. Sunset here is legendary.

ideas.title: Sella Pass
ideas.desc: The heart of the Sella Ronda loop — switchbacks beneath the sheer Sassolungo and Sella massifs.

ideas.title: Pordoi Pass
ideas.desc: A 2,239m pass with a cable car to Sass Pordoi, the "terrace of the Dolomites," for a sea-of-peaks panorama.

ideas.title: Bernina Express
ideas.desc: A UNESCO railway spiralling over the Landwasser Viaduct and Bernina glaciers from Chur to Italian Tirano.

ideas.title: Glacier Express
ideas.desc: The "slowest express in the world" — 8 panoramic hours across 291 bridges between Zermatt and St Moritz.

ideas.title: Aletsch Glacier
ideas.desc: The largest glacier in the Alps — a 23km river of ice. Ride to Eggishorn or Bettmerhorn for the jaw-drop view.

ideas.title: Vršič Pass
ideas.desc: 50 cobbled hairpins over the Julian Alps from Kranjska Gora to the Soča valley — Slovenia's greatest drive.

ideas.title: Lago di Sorapis
ideas.desc: A milky-blue glacial lake under jagged spires — a 6km cliff-path hike from Passo Tre Croci. The colour is unreal.

ideas.title: Cinque Torri
ideas.desc: Five tilted towers ringed by an open-air WWI front-line museum of trenches and tunnels. Easy loop, big views.

ideas.title: Lagazuoi
ideas.desc: A cable car to 2,778m and a tunnel-laced WWI mountain, with one of the broadest Dolomite panoramas anywhere.

ideas.title: Cadini di Misurina
ideas.desc: A short scramble near Tre Cime to a viewpoint over a fence of impossibly slender spires — a photographer's grail.

ideas.title: Creux du Van
ideas.desc: A vast natural rock amphitheatre — a 160m cliff arc with ibex and a "Swiss Grand Canyon" rim walk.

ideas.title: Partnach Gorge
ideas.desc: A boardwalk threads a roaring 700m gorge of waterfalls and ice — dramatic in every season.

ideas.title: Soča Valley & Tolmin Gorge
ideas.desc: An impossibly turquoise alpine river through emerald gorges — rafting, WWI history, and the greenest water you'll see.

ideas.title: Ritten Earth Pyramids
ideas.desc: Eerie hoodoo spires capped with boulders above Bolzano, reached by a cable car and a narrow-gauge train.

ideas.title: Verona
ideas.desc: Roman arena, Juliet's balcony and aperitivo squares — an easy first or last night on the way to/from the depot.

ideas.title: Kitzbühel
ideas.desc: A chic medieval ski town below the saw-toothed Wilder Kaiser — pastel lanes, gondolas and alpine meadows.

ideas.title: Merano
ideas.desc: A spa town with palm-lined promenades and the world-class Trauttmansdorff botanical gardens under the peaks.

ideas.title: Brixen / Bressanone
ideas.desc: The oldest town in Tyrol — arcaded streets, a baroque cathedral, and a gateway to the Plose and Funes valleys.

ideas.title: Bergamo Città Alta
ideas.desc: A walled hilltop old town of Venetian ramparts and piazzas, reached by funicular — a stunning gateway near Milan.

ideas.title: Ljubljana
ideas.desc: A pocket-sized capital of dragon bridges, riverside cafés and a hilltop castle — car-free, green, and effortless.

ideas.title: Trieste
ideas.desc: A grand Habsburg seaport of Viennese cafés and a clifftop castle where the Alps meet the Adriatic.

ideas.title: Neuschwanstein Castle
ideas.desc: The fairytale castle that inspired Disney, perched over gorges and lakes in the Bavarian Alps.

ideas.title: Predjama Castle
ideas.desc: A Renaissance castle dramatically built into the mouth of a cave cliff — pair it with nearby Postojna.

ideas.title: Interlaken
ideas.desc: The adventure hub between two lakes under the Eiger-Mönch-Jungfrau — paragliding, lake steamers, and rail to the peaks.

ideas.title: Val di Funes
ideas.desc: The Santa Maddalena church on a meadow knoll under the Odle spires — one of the most photographed scenes in the Alps.

ideas.title: Marmolada Glacier
ideas.desc: Cable car to 3,265m on the Dolomites' highest peak, with a WWI tunnel museum inside the mountain.

ideas.title: Zugspitze
ideas.desc: Germany's highest summit at 2,962m — a cogwheel and cable car to a glacier, with a four-country panorama.

ideas.title: Eisriesenwelt Ice Cave
ideas.desc: The largest ice cave on earth — 42km of frozen halls in the mountain, below a clifftop medieval fortress.

ideas.title: Postojna Cave
ideas.desc: An underground railway glides deep into a karst cavern of cathedral chambers and rare "baby dragon" olms.

ideas.title: Swarovski Crystal Worlds
ideas.desc: A surreal crystal art park behind a grass-covered giant's head — playful, weird, and great on a rainy day.

ideas.title: Eagle's Nest (Kehlsteinhaus)
ideas.desc: A mountaintop lodge at 1,834m reached by a brass-lined elevator bored through the rock — a sweeping panorama.

ideas.title: Dachstein Ice Cave & Skywalk
ideas.desc: Frozen caverns and a "Stairway to Nothingness" platform over a 400m drop — a dramatic add to a Hallstatt day.

ideas.title: Plitvice Lakes
ideas.desc: Sixteen terraced turquoise lakes linked by waterfalls and boardwalks — a bigger swing south, but a UNESCO stunner.

ideas.title: Miramare Castle
ideas.desc: A white fairytale castle on a sea promontory with botanical gardens — where the Alps melt into the Adriatic.

ideas.title: Hohenwerfen Castle
ideas.desc: A 900-year clifftop fortress with daily falconry shows, perched above the Eisriesenwelt ice cave.

ideas.title: Hohensalzburg Fortress
ideas.desc: One of Europe's largest fully-intact medieval fortresses, crowning the Salzburg skyline over the old town.

ideas.title: Tyrol Castle
ideas.desc: The ancestral castle that gave the whole region its name, on a vineyard ridge above Merano.

ideas.title: Bled Castle
ideas.desc: A clifftop medieval castle over Bled's turquoise lake and island church — the ultimate Slovenian postcard.

ideas.title: Bad Gastein
ideas.desc: A Belle-Époque spa town built around a waterfall crashing through its centre — grand hotels and radon-thermal baths.

ideas.title: Leukerbad
ideas.desc: The Alps' largest thermal resort — open-air pools steaming under 600m cliffs, reachable by mountain road.

ideas.title: Bad Ragaz & Tamina Gorge
ideas.desc: Soak in 36°C spring water piped straight from a dramatic slot gorge — wellness plus an easy gorge walk.

ideas.title: Bormio Terme
ideas.desc: Roman-era thermal baths at the foot of the Stelvio Pass, with panoramic outdoor pools over the valley.

ideas.title: Vintgar Gorge
ideas.desc: A wooden walkway clings to a cliff above the rushing emerald Radovna river — 1.6km of pure drama.

ideas.title: Peričnik Falls
ideas.desc: A 52m waterfall you can actually walk behind, in the wild Vrata valley below Mt Triglav.

ideas.title: Krimml Waterfalls
ideas.desc: Europe's tallest at 380m — a misty forest trail switchbacks beside three thundering tiers.

ideas.title: Liechtensteinklamm
ideas.desc: A slot gorge so narrow the walls nearly touch, with a glowing turquoise river churning far below.

ideas.title: Triglav National Park
ideas.desc: Slovenia's only national park — the Julian Alps, the Soča river and Lake Bohinj, all crowned by Mt Triglav.

ideas.title: Hohe Tauern National Park
ideas.desc: The Alps' largest protected area — glaciers, the Grossglockner, marmots and a wall of 3,000m peaks.

ideas.title: Stelvio National Park
ideas.desc: Italy's largest alpine park around the Stelvio Pass — glaciers, ibex and high-mountain trails.

ideas.title: Gran Paradiso National Park
ideas.desc: Italy's oldest national park, a former royal hunting reserve full of ibex below a 4,000m peak.

ideas.title: South Tyrolean Wine Road
ideas.desc: Vineyard villages south of Bolzano — Kaltern, Tramin, Appiano — for Gewürztraminer tastings and autumn Törggelen feasts.

ideas.title: Trento & Trentodoc
ideas.desc: An elegant Renaissance city and home of Italy's best metodo-classico sparkling wine — cellar tours and piazzas.

items.t: Swiss vignette (CHF 40) — buy at the border
Austria vignette (~€12) — sort before the border
Book paragliding (Lauterbrunnen) + Tre Cime toll road
Download offline maps for the whole route
Van: check height/length for Stelvio & tunnels
